review_summary: The transcript and unit summary provide enough grounded detail to generate 2 conceptual reinforcement questions about perplexity for evaluating language models without needing a separate video clip.

question_bank.question: What does perplexity measure for a language model?
choices: How well it predicts held-out human text
How quickly it trains on a GPU
How often it generates grammatical sentences during decoding
question_bank.explanation: Perplexity is used on held-out text to summarize next-word prediction quality. Lower perplexity means the model assigns higher probability to the observed text.

question_bank.question: Why should two perplexity numbers only be compared carefully?
choices: Because perplexity depends on the logarithm base used
Because perplexity can only be computed for n-gram models
Because perplexity ignores probabilities and uses only ranks
Because perplexity is meaningful only on training data
question_bank.explanation: Perplexity is an exponentiated log-loss, so the numerical value depends on which log base is used. Two reported values are only directly comparable when they use the same convention.
